stimulus: When a particular sector—rail transit and natural gas distribution are two familiar examples—requires extensive land rights and other regulatory permissions that deter newcomers, ______blank typically operates as a single firm per region. Such markets are referred to as regulated monopolies.
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?
rationale:
Choice B is the best answer. The convention being tested is pronoun-antecedent agreement. The singular pronoun "it" agrees in number with the singular antecedent "sector" and clearly indicates that the sector typically operates as a single firm per region.

Choice A is incorrect because the singular pronoun "this" is ambiguous in this context; the resulting sentence leaves unclear what typically operates as a single firm per region. Choice C is incorrect because the plural pronoun "they" doesn't agree in number with the singular antecedent "sector." Choice D is incorrect. The plural pronoun "these" neither agrees in number with the singular antecedent "sector" nor clearly indicates that the sector—not another plural noun in the sentence, such as "land rights" or "permissions"—typically operates as a single firm per region.
